title: Feminism
questions: What is feminism?
Why isn't there something called 'masculinism'?
Is your country totally fair and equal to women and men?
What do you know about the history of feminism?
What have feminist movements achieved in your country?
Do you think feminism sometimes has an unfair image?
Why do you think relatively few women belong to feminist movements?
Can men be feminists?
What important things should men know about feminism?
Why is there a stereotype of feminists hating men?
Do you think feminist movements are needed in this century?
How wide is the gender gap in your country?
What do you think feminists mean by 'reproductive freedom'?
What is your opinion of 'sexual objectification' - portraying women (or men) as objects of sexual desire?
What is the biggest worldwide problem facing women?
What problems are there for women in other countries that do not exist in your country?
What do you think of the view that marriage constitutes slavery for women, and that freedom for women cannot be won without the abolition of marriage?
There is a site called "Ladies Against Feminism," which "promotes beautiful womanhood." What do you think this is all about?
How would the world change if there was perfect sexual equality?
Which woman has made the greatest progress for women?
